coaching.coaching: Plan how verification commands for all AC items will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: verification commands for all AC items

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
verify command `wc -l` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `wc -l src/rebar/llm/anthropic_model.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `wc -l src/rebar/llm/anthropic_model.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `wc -l src/rebar/llm/runner.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `wc -l src/rebar/llm/runner.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/interfaces/store/test_execution_mode_dispatch.py` and `tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_framework.py` to the AC6 pytest invocation so the re-export and monkeypatch-seam contracts are directly verified.
evidence: Plan body: 'tests/interfaces/store/test_execution_mode_dispatch.py and test_llm_framework.py which access/monkeypatch the re-exported names'
AC6 lists only 4 test files, both interfaces/store tests are absent
findings.finding: AC6's explicit pytest command omits test_execution_mode_dispatch.py and test_llm_framework.py, both of which monkeypatch or directly import the re-exported symbols on runner_mod. The plan body names them as covered consumers but they are not exercised by the AC6 verification step, leaving the re-export contract unproven for these two files.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (`python -m pytest tests/unit/test_transport_retry.py tests/unit/test_llm_timeouts.py tests/unit/test_runner_local_proxy_bypass.py tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py -q`)
scenarios: If a re-export is accidentally omitted from runner.py, AC6 passes (the 4 unit tests still import the symbol fine) but test_execution_mode_dispatch.py would fail — the AC6 gate would not catch the regression.
If monkeypatch.setattr on runner_mod no longer intercepts the call (e.g., runner.py calls the symbol via the new module's namespace instead of its own), only these two tests would reveal it.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC6's command with: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_transport_retry.py tests/unit/test_llm_timeouts.py tests/unit/test_runner_local_proxy_bypass.py tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py tests/interfaces/store/test_execution_mode_dispatch.py tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_framework.py -q`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for the arch.md row deletion to AC4, e.g. `grep -c 'llm/runner' docs/architecture.md` returning 0 (or a `grep -L` / `grep -v` assertion), so that AC is fully verifiable in-session.
evidence: AC5 (`test_module_size_contract.py`) verifies 'allowlist == computed over-cap set == ceilings keys' but contains no assertion about docs/architecture.md
No grep or structured check is listed that would confirm absence of the runner.py row in the arch.md offenders table
findings.finding: AC4 bundles three distinct file changes (allowlist.txt, ceilings.txt, docs/architecture.md offenders-table row) but provides no proving command for the arch.md row deletion; AC5's pytest only validates the allowlist/ceilings consistency, leaving the arch.md change unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'runner.py graduated off the gate: its line removed from .github/module-size-allowlist.txt and .github/module-size-ceilings.txt, and its docs/architecture.md offenders-table remedy row deleted'
scenarios: An executor removes the row from allowlist/ceilings but forgets arch.md; all ACs pass green yet the table row persists — the stated end-state is not fully reached.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: `(verify: grep -c 'llm/runner' docs/architecture.md reports 0 matching rows)`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive entry for `src/rebar/llm/anthropic_model.py` (scope: provider/model resolution + Anthropic model construction) to `docs/architecture.md`'s llm subpackage section or module table, alongside the removal of the remedy row.
evidence: Plan says 'this ticket updates/removes the arch.md row accordingly' but specifies only deletion, not addition
AC item 4: 'its docs/architecture.md offenders-table remedy row deleted' — no corresponding add/update step
The Seam section describes the new module's contents narratively but only in the plan, not in any persistent doc artifact
findings.finding: The plan deletes the arch.md offenders-table remedy row for runner.py (AC item 4) but does not add a positive module-description entry for the new permanent module anthropic_model.py. If arch.md contains a module inventory or narrative descriptions of the llm/ subpackage's components, the new leaf will be entirely undocumented there after the row deletion, leaving future maintainers without a canonical description of its scope and role.
findings.location: AC item 4 / 'docs/architecture.md offenders-table remedy row deleted' / 'Seam' section
scenarios: A future developer looking at docs/architecture.md for the llm/ subpackage sees runner.py described (or its old entry gone) but finds no entry for anthropic_model.py
Someone trying to understand what belongs in anthropic_model.py vs runner.py has no canonical reference after the remedy row is removed
findings.suggested_fix: In the arch.md edit, after removing the offenders-table remedy row, add a one- or two-sentence module description entry for anthropic_model.py in whatever module-inventory or narrative section covers the llm/ subpackage, e.g.: '`anthropic_model.py` — provider/model resolution and Anthropic client construction (httpx timeout/transport wiring, retry config, cache settings); imported by `runner.py`, no back-import.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py` exists (or will be created by this story); if the file is missing, either create it or correct AC5 to reference the actual test that validates allowlist/ceilings sync.
evidence: search_files for 'test_module_size_contract' returns no results anywhere in the repo tree
Plan text: 'python -m pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q passes (allowlist == computed over-cap set == ceilings keys)'
The file is not present under tests/unit/ (list_directory and search both confirm absence)
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 references `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py` as the authoritative gate that the allowlist == computed over-cap set == ceilings keys, but this file does not exist anywhere in the repository. The `search_files` tool returns zero hits for 'test_module_size_contract'. An AC step that names a non-existent test file as its oracle cannot pass.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q` passes
scenarios: Running AC5 as written would fail immediately with 'ERROR: file or directory not found: tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py', making the graduation check unverifiable.
If the intent is that this test should be created as part of the story, the plan omits that work item entirely.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
